questions.question: What does max-width: 800px do?
options: Sets the width to exactly 800px
Sets the maximum width; element can be smaller
Sets the minimum width
Limits the width to never be under 800px
questions.explanation: max-width sets an upper limit on width. The element can be smaller than this value on narrow screens.

questions.question: Why should you pair width: 100% with max-width?
options: It's required syntax
Without width: 100%, the element won't shrink on small screens
It centers the element
It makes the element responsive to height
questions.explanation: width: 100% allows the element to shrink on small screens; max-width caps the growth on large screens.

questions.question: What does max-width: 65ch accomplish?
options: Sets width to 65 pixels
Sets the optimal line length for readability (about 65 characters)
Sets width to 65%
Sets max width to 65 viewport widths
questions.explanation: The ch unit equals the width of the '0' character. 65ch is approximately 65 characters per line — the ideal for readability.

questions.question: What happens when width is larger than max-width?
options: width wins and max-width is ignored
max-width overrides width and limits the element
The browser uses the average of both values
An error is thrown
questions.explanation: max-width overrides width when the computed width exceeds the max-width value.

questions.question: What does max-width: 100% do for images?
options: Makes the image exactly 100px wide
Prevents the image from exceeding its container width
Stretches the image to fill the screen
Makes the image 100% of its original size
questions.explanation: max-width: 100% on images ensures they never overflow their container, scaling down on smaller screens.

questions.question: How do you center an element with max-width?
options: max-width: center
max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto
max-width: 800px; text-align: center
center: max-width 800px
questions.explanation: Combine max-width with margin: 0 auto to create a centered, responsive container.

questions.question: What is the relationship between min-width and max-width when both are set?
options: The element stays between both values, respecting both constraints
min-width is ignored when max-width is set
max-width is ignored when min-width is set
The values are added together
questions.explanation: When both are set, the element will be at least min-width and at most max-width, adapting within that range.

questions.question: What's the difference between width: 100% and max-width: 100%?
options: They are identical
width: 100% forces the element to be 100%; max-width: 100% allows smaller sizes
max-width: 100% forces the element to be 100%
width: 100% only works on images
questions.explanation: width: 100% always fills the container. max-width: 100% prevents overflow but allows smaller sizing.

questions.question: What is a common use case for max-width on a container div?
options: Creating a fixed-width layout
Creating a responsive container that works on all screen sizes
Hiding content on mobile
Adding borders
questions.explanation: max-width on containers creates fluid layouts that expand to a limit on large screens and shrink on small ones.

questions.question: What does max-width: 1200px; width: 100%; achieve?
options: A fixed 1200px width that doesn't change
A flexible container that fills small screens but caps at 1200px on large ones
A container that is always 100% width regardless of screen size
A container that is 1200px wide on all devices
questions.explanation: This pattern creates a container that's fluid on small screens but constrained to 1200px on large screens.

questions.question: What is 'min-width' used for in responsive design?
options: Setting a fixed minimum size to prevent elements from becoming too small
Setting the preferred width
Setting the maximum width
Centering the element
questions.explanation: min-width sets a lower bound on element width, preventing it from shrinking below a usable size.

questions.question: Can max-width be specified in percentage?
options: Yes, max-width accepts percentages, viewport units, and all length units
No, max-width only accepts pixel values
Yes, but only for images
No, percentages are invalid for max-width
questions.explanation: max-width accepts all length units including px, em, rem, %, vw, ch, etc.

questions.question: How does the browser prioritize min-width, width, and max-width?
options: The browser uses min-width > width > max-width in priority order
The browser uses the most restrictive value
The browser adds them together
The last declared property wins
questions.explanation: Priority: min-width wins if larger than width; max-width wins if smaller than width. The actual width is constrained between min and max.

questions.question: What does max-inline-size refer to?
options: The maximum height of an element
The maximum size in the inline direction (logical property for max-width)
The maximum size of an inline element
The maximum line height
questions.explanation: max-inline-size is the logical property equivalent of max-width, adapting to writing direction.

questions.question: What is the purpose of max-width in media queries?
options: To define the maximum width of a media feature
To apply styles when the viewport is AT or BELOW a certain width
To set the maximum width of the viewport
To limit which devices the styles apply to
questions.explanation: In media queries, max-width acts as an upper bound: @media (max-width: 768px) applies styles at 768px and below.

questions.question: Can max-width be used on inline elements?
options: Yes, max-width works on all elements including inline
No, inline elements ignore width properties
Yes, but only on replaced inline elements like images
No, max-width only works on block elements
questions.explanation: Regular inline elements ignore width-based properties including max-width, but replaced inline elements (images, inputs) respect them.

questions.question: What is the initial/default value of max-width?
options: none
auto
questions.explanation: The default value of max-width is 'none', meaning no upper limit is imposed on the element's width.

questions.question: How does max-width interact with box-sizing: border-box?
options: max-width is calculated including padding and border
max-width is calculated on content only
max-width is ignored with border-box
max-width doubles when border-box is used
questions.explanation: max-width includes padding and border in its limit when box-sizing: border-box is applied.

questions.question: Why is max-width considered essential for responsive design?
options: It's the only way to set width in CSS
It allows elements to adapt to different screen sizes without media queries
It automatically centers all content
It prevents horizontal scrolling
questions.explanation: max-width creates baked-in responsiveness — elements automatically adapt to available space without requiring media queries.

questions.question: What happens when max-width is smaller than min-width?
options: The browser uses max-width
The element becomes unresponsive
The browser uses min-width (it's more restrictive)
The element uses both values averaged
questions.explanation: When max-width conflicts with min-width, the browser uses the more restrictive value — min-width wins.
